zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John de Sutton 1st Lord Sutton of Holderness於1270年6月24日出生在英國越可爾霍爾德內斯時，他的父親Saire Sutton26歲，母親Anne de Ros40歲。他於1294年8月27日與Constance Sampson在英國英格蘭越可爾克利夫蘭結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1338年9月24日在英國武斯特斯爾享年68歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John de Sutton 1st Lord Sutton of Holderness于1270年6月24日出生在英国越可爾霍爾德內斯时，他的父亲Saire Sutton26岁，母亲Anne de Ros40岁。他于1294年8月27日与Constance Sampson在英国英格兰越可爾克利夫蘭结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1338年9月24日在英国武斯特斯爾享年68岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Sutton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called Sutton, named with Old English sūth ‘south, southern’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The English surname is also common in Ireland (Wexford, Kildare), where it has been established since the 13th century and Gaelicized as de Sutún.
Jewish (from the former Ottoman Empire, including Syria): unexplained.
English: topographic name from Middle English (bi) suthentune ‘(at the place to the) south of the village’.

variants.name: Setton
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Avi, Ronit, Yossef.  French Andree, Henri, Jacques, Maryse.
Jewish (from the Ottoman Empire): altered form of   Sutton  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John de Sutton 1st Lord Sutton of Holderness was born on 24 June 1270, in Holderness, Yorkshire, England, his father, Saire Sutton, was 26 and his mother, Anne de Ros, was 40. He married Constance Sampson on 27 August 1294, in Cleveland, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 24 September 1338, in Worcestershire, England, at the age of 68.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John de Sutton 1st Lord Sutton of Holderness est né le 24 juin 1270, à Holderness, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Saire Sutton, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Anne de Ros, avait 40 ans. Il a épousé Constance Sampson le 27 août 1294, à Cleveland, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 24 septembre 1338, à Worcestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 68 ans.

Er hat am 27. August 1294 in Cleveland, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom Constance Sampson geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 24. ja.lifeSummary: Sir John de Sutton 1st Lord Sutton of Holdernessが1270年6月24日にイギリスヨークシュアイアホールデルネスで生まれた時，父Saire Suttonは26歳，母Anne de Rosは40歳であった。1294年8月27日にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアクレベランドでConstance Sampsonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1338年9月24日にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアで68歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John de Sutton 1st Lord Sutton of Holderness(이)가 1270년 6월 24일 영국 요르크실르 홀더네스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Saire Sutton(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Anne de Ros(은)는 40세였다. 그는 1294년 8월 27일 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 클레벨랜드에서 Constance Sampson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1338년 9월 24일 영국 워스스터실르에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Sutton On Trent, Nottinghamshire, Englan: суттон он трент, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John de Sutton 1st Lord Sutton of Holderness родился 24 июня 1270 г. в местности холдирнесс, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Saire Sutton было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne de Ros, 40. Он женился на женщине по имени Constance Sampson 27 августа 1294 г. в местности кливеланд, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 24 сентября 1338 г. в местности ворсестиршир, Англия в возрасте 68 лет.
